title: baldellia repens subsp. baetica
intro: Baldellia repens subsp. baetica, also known as Baetic Baldellia or Baldellia of Andalusia, is a rare, aquatic plant species belonging to the Alismataceae family, native to the southwestern region of Spain. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, to be used for therapeutic purposes. However, due to its rarity and limited scientific research, further studies are necessary to fully understand and confirm its medicinal properties and health benefits.
